ops.text: cookiesync bridge live-Chrome tests (internal/bridge TestBridgeSeedAndServe + internal/daemon TestBridgeOpenReattachClose/TestBridgeFreshOpensDoNotShareSecrets) each launch a real Chrome over --remote-debugging-pipe with a 10s handshake deadline. Under `go test ./...` the two packages run concurrently (plus any co-resident Chrome, e.g. a live codex/agent-browser session), and Chrome cold-start under that contention exceeds 10s → the tests false-fail with "cdp handshake ... context deadline exceeded" (empty stderr, since no --enable-logging). In isolation each passes in ~2s. Two fixes: bump the Launch handshake timeout well above 10s (a loaded Mac at real bridge-open can also exceed it), and run the Chrome-launching packages with `-p 1` (or a shared resource lock) so they don't contend. Also note: piping `go test` through `| tail`/`| head` masks the non-zero exit — read the FAIL lines, not the pipe status.
